type CacheLifeConfig = {
  expire?: number
}

/**
 * This function allows you to purge [cached data](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/caching) on-demand for a specific cache tag.
 *
 * Read more: [Next.js Docs: `revalidateTag`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/revalidateTag)
 */
export function revalidateTag(tag: string, profile: string | CacheLifeConfig) {
  if (!profile) {
    console.warn(
      '"revalidateTag" without the second argument is now deprecated, add second argument of "max" or use "updateTag". See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/revalidate-tag-single-arg'
    )
  }
  return revalidate([tag], `revalidateTag ${tag}`, profile)
}

export function revalidateTag(tag: string, profile: string | CacheLifeConfig) {
  if (!profile) {
    console.warn(
      '"revalidateTag" without the second argument is now deprecated, add second argument of "max" or use "updateTag". See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/revalidate-tag-single-arg'
    )
  }
  return revalidate([tag], `revalidateTag ${tag}`, profile)
}

1	// Simulated in-memory product catalog.
2	// In production this would query a real database; the module-level state
3	// is updated asynchronously by syncCatalog() to simulate upstream changes.
4	let products = [
5	  { id: 1, sku: 'LAP-001', name: 'UltraBook Pro 16',       price: 1999, inventory: 12,  category: 'Laptops',    status: 'active',       lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
6	  { id: 2, sku: 'PHN-023', name: 'Galaxy S30',              price: 999,  inventory: 48,  category: 'Phones',     status: 'active',       lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
7	  { id: 3, sku: 'TAB-007', name: 'Tab Ultra 14',            price: 749,  inventory: 0,   category: 'Tablets',    status: 'discontinued', lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
8	  { id: 4, sku: 'AUD-112', name: 'Noise-Cancelling Pro',    price: 349,  inventory: 235, category: 'Audio',      status: 'active',       lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
9	  { id: 5, sku: 'WCH-034', name: 'SportWatch 5',            price: 399,  inventory: 88,  category: 'Wearables',  status: 'active',       lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
10	  { id: 6, sku: 'MON-019', name: '4K HDR Monitor 32"',      price: 1299, inventory: 31,  category: 'Monitors',   status: 'active',       lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
11	  { id: 7, sku: 'KEY-055', name: 'Mechanical Keyboard TKL', price: 179,  inventory: 0,   category: 'Accessories', status: 'backorder',    lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
12	  { id: 8, sku: 'MSE-022', name: 'Ergo Trackball',          price: 89,   inventory: 412, category: 'Accessories', status: 'active',       lastUpdated: '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z' },
13	];
14	
15	export async function getAllProducts() {
16	  // Simulate a lightweight database read
17	  await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 80));
18	  return products;
19	}
20	
21	/**
22	 * Simulates a long-running catalog sync from an upstream ERP/PIM system.
23	 * Updates pricing, inventory, and availability in the background.
24	 *
25	 * In production this would enqueue a job (e.g. via a message queue / job
26	 * worker) and return immediately.  The worker processes thousands of SKUs
27	 * and eventually updates the database, after which cache revalidation
28	 * ensures downstream views see fresh data.
29	 */
30	export async function syncCatalog() {
31	  // Simulate the network / compute latency of a real sync job
32	  await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 3000));
33	
34	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
35	
36	  // Mutate pricing and inventory to simulate an upstream data refresh
37	  products = products.map(p => {
38	    const priceDelta = Math.round(Math.random() * 60 - 20);   // -20 .. +40
39	    const invDelta   = Math.round(Math.random() * 30 - 8);     // -8  .. +22
40	    return {
41	      ...p,
42	      price:       Math.max(1, p.price + priceDelta),
43	      inventory:   Math.max(0, p.inventory + invDelta),
44	      lastUpdated: now,
45	    };
46	  });
47	
48	  // Occasionally simulate a new SKU arriving from upstream
49	  if (Math.random() > 0.6) {
50	    const id  = products.length + 1;
51	    const now = new Date().toISOString();
52	    products = [
53	      ...products,
54	      {
55	        id,
56	        sku: `NEW-SKU-${String(id).padStart(3, '0')}`,
57	        name: `Catalog Item ${id}`,
58	        price:       Math.round(Math.random() * 1500 + 50),
59	        inventory:   Math.round(Math.random() * 200),
60	        category:    ['Accessories', 'Audio', 'Monitors'][Math.floor(Math.random() * 3)],
61	        status:      'active',
62	        lastUpdated: now,
63	      },
64	    ];
65	  }
66	}
